title: Messaging Outcomes and Delivery Evidence in Go · Language Lens: Delivery Guarantee Claim
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must separate protocol custody from application effects with explicit structs and table-driven outcomes; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the delivery guarantee claim increment in a typed outcome and ownership evidence record. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Delivery Guarantee Claim · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 9023 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate local publication, broker custody, and observable stakeholder effect; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Delivery Guarantee Claim contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Delivery Guarantee Claim · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Predict Delivery Guarantee Claim · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim, keep Delivery Guarantee Claim connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name publisher intent, topology and route evidence, connection and channel generation, notification and goroutine ownership, message identity, byte, time, attempt, prefetch, and in-flight budgets, terminal delivery decision, durable effect, cancellation, cleanup, and recovery; run a pure deterministic Go model; then change one message or failure and state the Go compiler, race, leak, amqp091-go, RabbitMQ, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, or production transfer gate. Apply that move specifically to Delivery Guarantee Claim while producing a typed outcome and ownership evidence record.
